channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he's joining Bessent and Lutnick for trade negotiations with Japanese at the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said he’s joining some of his top economic advisers on Wednesday for negotiations at the White House over tariffs and trade with a top Japanese official who is traveling to Washington for the talks.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration sues Maine over participation of transgender athletes in girls sports
item.description: The Trump administration is suing Maine’s education department for not complying with its push to ban transgender athletes in girls and women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Easter Monday could soon become federal holiday under new GOP proposal
item.description: Two GOP lawmakers in the House and Senate are leading the way on a new legislative proposal that could soon make Easter Monday a federal holiday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Older Arizona voters are closely watching Trump's tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump was elected with a promise to improve the economy, lower taxes and control inflation, addressing voters who said the economy was the country's top issue.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas poised to create its own version of DOGE as bill passes both chambers
item.description: Texas lawmakers passed a bill that would establish a new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) following Elon Musk's federal model. The state agency will focus on cutting bureaucracy and improving government services.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers press Hope Florida officials over political contributions
item.description: Lawmakers questioned Hope Florida officials Tuesday morning to further investigate those involved in the ongoing probe into the non-profit and a mysterious money trail tied to a $10 million Medicaid settlement.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Border deployment vehicle accident kills two service members, third in critical condition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two service members deployed to the U.S. Southern border were killed and a third is in serious condition after a vehicle accident near Santa Teresa, New Mexico, the military announced late Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOT Sec Sean Duffy finalizes $150M grant to build new port of entry on southern border
item.description: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy announced Tuesday that his department had finalized an agreement for a $150 million grant to build a new entry port along the California border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden recalls seeing 'colored kids' on segregated bus during his youth in post-presidency speech
item.description: Former President Joe Biden referred to Black students as "colored kids" while discussing how schools were segregated in Delaware while he was growing up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon deputy chief of staff is second Hegseth advisor removed amid DOD leak probe
item.description: Pentagon deputy chief of staff Darin Selnick was removed from the Department of Defense headquarters on Tuesday, according to an official who confirmed the matter with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department can cut funding for legal guidance for people facing deportation, US judge says
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday allowed the Justice Department to temporarily stop funding legal education programs for people facing deportation or immigration court while a lawsuit brought by the organizations that provide the service moves forward in court. The decision from U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House quietly floats millionaire tax hike proposal in Congress as GOP leaders signal opposition
item.description: House Republicans are looking for ways to pass President Donald Trump's policies via the budget reconciliation process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has dug in on its contention that the government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite U.S. Supreme Court and lower court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Soaring Medicare prescription drug prices targeted in Trump's new executive order
item.description: The order instructs the Department of Health and Human Services to standardize Medicare payments for prescription drugs no matter where a patient receives treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge orders federal agencies to release billions of dollars from two Biden-era initiatives
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to release billions of dollars meant to finance climate and infrastructure projects across the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge orders Trump officials' depositions, blasts inaction in Abrego Garcia case
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — A federal judge Tuesday ordered depositions from several Trump officials, blasting the administration for failing to take any steps to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongly deported to a Salvadoran prison. During a hearing, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marshall man claims ICE detained him for protests over police killings
item.description: A Marshall man was detained by federal immigration authorities who claim he overstayed his student visa after it was revoked. However, he tells MPR News from the Kandiyohi County Jail that his arrest may have been politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump assassination plot part of domestic terrorism rise: Ex-FBI
item.description: Ex-FBI special agent Stuart Kaplan joined "NewsNation Now" to discuss how it is increasing across the United States, largely thanks to social media.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders, AOC hold rally 'Fighting Oligarchy' in Bakersfield
item.description: Sanders and Ocasio-Cortez are set for an event in Bakersfield decrying President Donald Trump's policies and economic disparities.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: 281 bills fall victim to deadline at Nevada Legislature. Here are the highlights
item.description: A total of 281 bills missed a Friday deadline to win committee passage in Carson City, the first major deadline at the Nevada Legislature this session. If that seems like a lot, consider that 875 bills are still alive.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harvard rejects Trump demands for funding
item.description: Harvard University on Monday rejected demands from the Trump administration as it threatens the school's federal funding as part of a broader clampdown on higher education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders jokingly calls Ocasio-Cortez his daughter at Utah rally
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) joked during a joint appearance at a Utah rally on Sunday that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) was his daughter, praising the progressive lawmaker and highlighting her background.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans vying for open Minnesota Senate seat laud conservative values, support for Trump
item.description: The pool of Republican state Senate candidates in north-central Minnesota includes some familiar names in state politics who joined the special election sprint. The GOP primary is April 15.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: President Donald Trump said Congress should “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: A retrial of Sarah Palin's case against 'New York Times' begins Monday
item.description: Former Alaska Gov. Sarah Palin has the rare opportunity to retry her defamation case against The New York Times even though she lost it — twice in a 24-hour-period — in early 2022.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from ‘reciprocal’ tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence. It began gaining momentum after the Supreme Court’s 2022 ruling overturning Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China put steep tariffs on U.S. exports. Farmers are worried
item.description: The first Trump administration spent $28 billion bailing out farmers during a trade war with China. The White House has said it's starting to look at how to help this time around.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: GOP ethics complaint alleges conflict by DFL Senate President Bobby Joe Champion
item.description: Six GOP lawmakers said the Minneapolis Democrat broke Senate rules when he carried legislation funding the nonprofit of a former legal client.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News
